rationale.edition: All changed files live under web/src/ — the OSS frontend tree. No paths touch enterprise-owned directories (enterprise/, o2_enterprise/, src/enterprise/), and no enterprise-gated keywords (SSO, SAML, RBAC, SDR, cipher, logo-management) appear in the changed paths. This is a pure open-source UI change.
rationale.skip: No explicit opt-out marker was provided: trigger_comment.txt and feature_hint.txt are both empty. The diff touches multiple user-facing Vue components (new RUM sessions sub-components, a redesigned sessions view with interactive segment filters and a KPI metrics strip), so it is clearly user-facing and not skipped.
rationale.e2e: The RUM AppSessions.vue view received a sweeping redesign: 4 new sub-components (SessionsMetricsStrip with clickable KPI cards, SessionsInsightBanner with action buttons, SessionActivitySparkline with lazy-loaded sparklines, SessionHealthCell with error/frustration/clean tags), 3 new segment toggle groups (health, type, device), restructured table columns (session, activity, health, duration), and a filtered-empty state. These are surface-level user interactions — card clicks, segment toggling, filter resets, banner actions, table rendering — that need verification against a real backend.
rationale.area: Every new and modified file that carries the feature logic sits under web/src/components/rum/sessionReplay/ and web/src/views/RUM/. The existing playwright.yml groups have a dedicated RUM testfolder shard (with sourcemap-ui.spec.js), making RUM the natural home.
rationale.group: The playwright.yml matrix already defines a RUM testfolder group (line 532) that currently runs sourcemap-ui.spec.js. This spec joins that shard — it is the only RUM group available, and adding a new sessions-focused spec there keeps RUM coverage colocated without creating a new shard.
rationale.existing_tests: No Playwright E2E specs (tests/ui-testing/playwright-tests/) were changed in the diff. The only test files modified are Vitest unit tests — SessionLocationColumn.spec.ts and AppSessions.spec.ts — which live in web/src/ and exercise components in isolation, not as E2E browser flows. These do not constitute existing E2E coverage.
